label: PyNode Bridge (PNB1)
info:
Both directions of the PyNode <-> Node-RED UDP bridge (PNB1 protocol).

PyNode -> Node-RED: 'udp in' (port 7401) -> 'PNB1 reassemble' -> debug.
Node-RED -> PyNode: inject -> 'PNB1 chunk+send' -> 'udp out' (127.0.0.1:7402).

See README.md next to this file for setup instructions. Ports 7401/7402 are
placeholders matching examples/workflows/nodered_bridge.json - change them to
match your PyNode Node-RED In/Out node configuration.

name: PyNode -> Node-RED
info: PyNode's 'Node-RED Out' node sends PNB1 datagrams here. Configure the 'udp in' node's port to match that node's configured destination port (default 7401 in the example workflow).

name: PNB1 reassemble
Node-RED -> PyNode
info: Replace this inject node with whatever produces the message you want to send to PyNode. Configure the 'udp out' node's address/port to match the PyNode 'Node-RED In' node's bind host/port (default port 7402 in the example workflow).

name: PNB1 chunk+send

label: PyNode Bridge (TCP/NDJSON)
info: TCP/NDJSON transport: reliable, ordered, zero custom decode code. Pair with PyNode's 'Node-RED TCP Out' (default port 7403) and 'Node-RED TCP In' (default port 7404) nodes.

name: PyNode -> Node-RED (PyNode 'Node-RED TCP Out' connects here)
promote to msg (optional)
Node-RED -> PyNode (set tcp out host to the PyNode machine)
NDJSON stringify
